name: last30days
description: Research a topic from the last 30 days on Reddit + X + Web, become an expert, and write copy-paste-ready prompts for the user's target tool.
capabilities: Search Reddit and X for discussions from the last 30 days using official APIs
Enrich results with real engagement metrics (upvotes, comments, likes, reposts)
Filter and rank results by recency, relevance, and popularity scores
Synthesize findings into actionable insights and copy-paste prompts
Support WebSearch fallback when API keys are not configured
Cache results for 24 hours to reduce API calls and improve response time
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer detected 484 pattern matches, but 480+ are false positives from markdown documentation (backtick code blocks misidentified as shell execution) and test fixtures. The skill legitimately accesses user-configured API keys (OPENAI_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY) from ~/.config/last30days/.env and makes HTTP requests to Reddit/X APIs for research. No malicious intent detected. Risk limited to expected behavior for an API-based research tool.
valueStatement: Stay current with fast-moving AI trends without drowning in noise. This skill surfaces what people are actually upvoting and discussing right now, then writes copy-paste prompts tailored to your target tool.
userTitle: Research trending topics from the last 30 days
limitations: Requires OPENAI_API_KEY for Reddit data and XAI_API_KEY for X data (optional but recommended)
Limited to content from the last 30 days - not suitable for historical research
Output quality depends on available discussions in the target topic
Cannot access private or paywalled content from Reddit/X

useCases.title: Prompt Engineering Research
useCases.description: Research the latest prompting techniques for specific AI tools by analyzing what the community shares and upvotes

useCases.title: Product Recommendation Discovery
useCases.description: Find specific tools and products people recommend, ranked by engagement signals rather than SEO

useCases.title: Trend Monitoring
useCases.description: Track what is currently being discussed in fast-moving domains like AI tools, viral content, or news

promptTemplates.title: Basic Topic Research
promptTemplates.prompt: Research the best [topic] from the last 30 days and summarize what people are recommending
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick research for general topic understanding

promptTemplates.title: Tool-Specific Prompt Research
promptTemplates.prompt: Research [topic] techniques for [target tool] and write copy-paste prompts I can use
promptTemplates.scenario: Learning prompting techniques for a specific AI tool

promptTemplates.title: Comparative Product Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Find discussions comparing [product A] vs [product B] from the last 30 days on Reddit and X

promptTemplates.title: Deep Dive with All Sources
promptTemplates.prompt: Do comprehensive research on [topic] using Reddit, X, and web sources. Include engagement metrics and identify patterns across all three sources
promptTemplates.scenario: Thorough research requiring cross-validation of findings

bestPractices: Specify both topic and target tool for most relevant prompt recommendations
Review the engagement metrics to identify community-validated insights
Use the --deep flag for comprehensive research on important topics
antiPatterns: Do not expect historical data beyond the 30-day window
Do not use for topics with minimal online discussion
Do not skip reviewing the raw sources before acting on recommendations

faq.question: Do I need API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill works with WebSearch fallback when no keys are configured. However, adding OPENAI_API_KEY unlocks Reddit data with real upvotes and comments, while XAI_API_KEY unlocks X posts with likes and reposts.

faq.question: How does the skill enforce the 30-day window?
faq.answer: The skill passes date constraints to API searches and applies hard date filtering as a safety net. Items with verified dates outside the range are excluded from results.

faq.question: Can I cache results to avoid repeated API calls?
faq.answer: Yes. Results are automatically cached for 24 hours keyed by topic and date range. Use the --refresh flag to bypass cache and fetch fresh data.

faq.question: What if my topic has little recent discussion?
faq.answer: The skill will show a limited data warning and include older evergreen content. It is honest about sparse results rather than fabricating findings.

faq.question: Can other skills use the research output?
faq.answer: Yes. Output files are written to ~/.local/share/last30days/out/ and can be imported via --emit=context or --emit=json for programmatic use in other skills.

faq.question: Is my research topic sent to external APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. Your topic query is sent to OpenAI and xAI APIs for research. Do not include sensitive information in research topics.
